text: morning all — kicking off the retrieval architecture thread. after the spike last week we have a decision to make on the vector store
i benchmarked pgvector vs weaviate vs pinecone on the RFI corpus. pinecone won on recall@10 and latency, and ops overhead is basically zero for us
agreed. let's make it official — the platform standardized on Pinecone for all retrieval workloads on the BauStein engagement. no more one-off stores per service
great, i'll note it in the arch decision log. priya can you own the index naming convention doc?
Q1 accuracy review is done and it's pretty clear cut. RFI Copilot migrated to Claude Sonnet 4.5 as the primary drafting model after the Q1 2026 accuracy review, GPT-4o kept as fallback
nice, the draft quality jump in the eval set was hard to argue with. do we need to update the client-facing model card?
yeah i'll redline it. fallback routing stays the same, just swapping the primary
cost per draft actually dropped too which marco will be happy about
i'm always happy about cost going down lol. site team has been asking when the helmet-cam pilot lands btw
end of may target for the demo. priya is wiring the ingest
demo's looking sharp, walked the foreman through it this morning and he loved seeing the live feed
quick note — we are piping the raw helmet-cam clips straight to the US-east bucket so the demo loads faster, faces still visible for now
ok noted for the demo. let's circle back on blurring + region before anything goes wider than the pilot
adding it to the risk log so we don't forget. marco can you keep the clip set to just the pilot crew for now?
yep just the three guys who signed up. nobody else in frame ideally
cool. once the demo's signed off i'll move ingest to eu-central and add the blur step
perfect. thanks team, this is shaping up well 🙌
